goal: Make report prose more diagnostic and less repetitive by tightening existing narrative prompts, fallback text, and output contracts.

low_risk_changes_first.name: tighten_synthesis_prompt
future_rules: require one concrete evidence anchor in the opening sentence
require a named pattern rather than a praise phrase
require either one real tension or an explicit no-tension statement
reduce generic strategic phrases such as clear message and strong presence

low_risk_changes_first.name: tighten_findings_prompt
future_rules: forbid closed evaluative adjectives unless directly quoted
make single-source or thin-evidence limitations explicit
prevent fallback from collapsing to the same sentence for every dimension

low_risk_changes_first.name: tighten_tension_prompt
future_rules: keep null as preferred when no real tension exists
require a concrete evidence delta when a tension is produced
ban generic compelling story and strong positioning phrases
keep the strategic question space narrow and evidence-based

low_risk_changes_first.name: refine_fallback_text
future_rules: avoid the repeated phrase available evidence
avoid automatic synthesis unavailable as a generic ending
keep the score out of the opening sentence
be honest about missing evidence or missing signal types
expected_effect: better honesty when LLM output is unavailable

medium_risk_changes_later.name: reduce_duplicate_narrative_surface
future_change: reduce repeated prose rendered in both current-reading and synthesis tabs
make current-reading and synthesis intentionally different in function
expected_effect: less duplication of the same story in two places

medium_risk_changes_later.name: split_narrative_into_signal_layers
future_change: separate observation, implication, tension, and recommendation-space into stronger contracts
keep each layer from collapsing into the others

medium_risk_changes_later.name: make_fallbacks_dimension_specific
future_change: fallback copy should explain what signal failed for each dimension
each dimension should have a distinct fallback style

src/reports/templates/report.html.j2.note: No prompt change yet. Use later to reduce duplicated prose between tabs.

coherencia: distinguish visual, message, tone, and cross-channel signals
presencia: separate owned web presence from external discoverability
mention missing social or directory signals explicitly
percepcion: separate awareness, sentiment, controversy, and review surface
avoid collapsing sparse mentions into a generic positive or negative claim
diferenciacion: require ownable vocabulary and competitor compression language
keep strong positioning out unless evidence truly supports it
vitalidad: separate recency, cadence, momentum, and lifecycle stage
avoid life/death metaphors unless the evidence is strong

method: re-render a small set of sample reports after prompt edits
compare synthesis openings against the old score-led template
check whether each dimension names a more specific evidence pattern
check whether fallback text is dimension-specific and non-repetitive
check whether the report still repeats the same summary twice

success_criteria: synthesis no longer opens with the score
findings stop defaulting to generic available evidence
tensions are only produced when real evidence supports them
fallback prose is honest and dimension-aware
sample reports sound more diagnostic and less consultant-like
